props.name: name
props.description: The agent's name — the primary label. Attribute: `name`.

props.name: active
props.description: Selected / focused state: highlighted border + surface. Attribute: `active`.

props.name: needsAttention
props.description: Raise a prominent "Needs you" pill plus a glowing amber edge — the attention-routing signal that pulls focus to this agent. Attribute: `needs-attention`.

props.name: status
props.type: undefined | { tone: "working" | "idle" | "done" | "error" | "blocked"; label?: undefined | string; pulse?: undefined | false | true }
props.description: Run status — a JS PROPERTY (object), not an attribute. Shape: `{ tone, label?, pulse? }`, where `tone` is one of `working` | `idle` | `done` | `error` | `blocked` (maps to the kit's tool hues), `label` is an optional short string beside the dot, and `pulse` animates the dot. Set it with `el.status = { tone: 'working', label: 'Working', pulse: true }`.
props.displayType: undefined | { tone: "working" | "idle" | "done" | "error" | "blocked"; label?: undefined | string; pulse?: undefined | false | true }

events.name: kai-activate
events.description: The card was activated — clicked, or Enter / Space while focused. Promote this agent back to focus.

events.name: kai-menu
events.description: The trailing "..." kebab was clicked. The consumer opens its own menu; the card only surfaces the affordance (the click does not also activate the card).

parts.name: status
parts.doc: The leading tone-colored status dot.

parts.name: menu
parts.doc: The trailing overflow ("...") menu button.

props.name: src
props.description: URL the preview iframe frames. Consumer-controlled.

props.name: files
props.type: { path: string; url?: undefined | string; code?: undefined | string; language?: undefined | string; type?: undefined | "html" | "pdf" | "image" | "other"; additions?: undefined | number; deletions?: undefined | number; status?: undefined | "added" | "modified" | "deleted" | "renamed" | "untracked" }[]
props.description: Files for the Code tab tree + each file's preview `url`. Set as a JS property (array).
props.typeShape: { path: string; url?: undefined | string; code?: undefined | string; language?: undefined | string; type?: undefined | "html" | "pdf" | "image" | "other"; additions?: undefined | number; deletions?: undefined | number; status?: undefined | "added" | "modified" | "deleted" | "renamed" | "untracked" }[]
props.displayType: { path: string; url?: undefined | string; code?: undefined | string; language?: undefined | string; type?: undefined | "html" | "pdf" | "image" | "other"; additions?: undefined | number; deletions?: undefined | number; status?: undefined | "added" | "modified" | "deleted" | "renamed" | "untracked" }[]

props.name: tab
props.description: Controlled active tab: `preview` or `code`. When set, the artifact follows it (re-asserted on change). Leave unset for an uncontrolled tab (see `defaultTab`).

props.name: defaultTab
props.description: Uncontrolled INITIAL tab (used only when `tab` is unset). Default `preview`. Seeds the starting tab; the user can then switch freely without the consumer re-asserting a controlled `tab`.

props.name: activeFile
props.description: Selected file path — syncs the tree highlight, Code source, and preview.

props.name: sandbox
props.description: iframe `sandbox` override. Secure default `allow-scripts allow-forms` (NOT `allow-same-origin`).

props.name: iframeTitle
props.description: Accessible title for the preview iframe.

props.name: maximized
props.description: Reflects the artifact's own maximized view-state (usually driven by the protocol).

props.name: expandable
props.description: Show the expand-to-fill button (OPT-IN).

props.name: openInTab
props.description: Show the open-in-new-tab button (OPT-IN).

props.name: noNav
props.description: Hide back/forward.

props.name: noReload
props.description: Hide reload.

props.name: noHome
props.description: Hide home.

props.name: noPathField
props.description: Hide the address field.

props.name: noTabs
props.description: Hide the Preview|Code toggle.

props.name: standalone
props.description: Standalone chrome: rounded corners + border (else square, borderless in-panel).

props.name: readonlyPath
props.description: Show the address but make it read-only (visible, nav-tracking, non-editable).

props.name: displayUrl
props.description: Friendly address shown in the path field instead of the real current url (read-only, non-navigable). Use when the framed url is not consumer-facing (e.g. a `data:` blob) so a clean address shows instead of leaking it. Scalar string: set as the `display-url` attribute or the `displayUrl` property.

events.name: kai-file-select
events.description: Fired when a file is selected. `detail.path`.

events.name: kai-maximize-change
events.description: Artifact's own maximize button toggled (consumer-observable; non-bubbling).

events.name: kai-navigate
events.description: Fired when the preview navigates. `detail.url` = the new location.

events.name: kai-tab-change
events.description: Fired when the Preview|Code tab changes. `detail.tab`.

methods.name: back
methods.description: Go back in the artifact's own history stack (no-op when there's no prior entry).

methods.name: forward
methods.description: Go forward in the history stack (no-op when there's no forward entry).

methods.name: reload
methods.description: Force-reload the current preview url (also re-renders an inline PDF).

methods.name: home
methods.description: Navigate to the `src` home url (no-op when there's no `src`).

methods.name: navigate
methods.description: Push + load a url in the preview — the path-field submit path (fires kai-navigate).

methods.name: selectFile
methods.description: Select a file by path: highlights the tree, shows its source, navigates the preview (fires kai-file-select + kai-navigate). Named selectFile to avoid the `activeFile` prop.

methods.name: openExternal
methods.description: Open the current url in a new browser tab (no-op when there's no concrete url). Named openExternal, NOT openInTab — that's a prop (toolbar button visibility).

methods.name: maximize
methods.description: Enter the maximized view-state (fires kai-maximize-change{maximized:true}). Named maximize, NOT maximized — that's a prop.

methods.name: restore
methods.description: Exit the maximized view-state (fires kai-maximize-change{maximized:false}).

props.name: items
props.type: { id: string; type: "file" | "source-document"; filename?: undefined | string; mediaType?: undefined | string; url?: undefined | string; title?: undefined | string }[]
props.description: The attachments to render. Set as a JS property (array).
props.typeShape: { id: string; type: "file" | "source-document"; filename?: undefined | string; mediaType?: undefined | string; url?: undefined | string; title?: undefined | string }[]

props.name: variant
props.description: Layout: `grid` = visual tiles, `inline` = icon + label chips, `list` = rows.

props.name: hoverCard
props.description: Wrap each item in a hover card that previews its details.

props.name: removable
props.description: Show a remove button per item; clicking it fires a `kai-remove` event.

props.name: showMediaType
props.description: Also show the media type beneath the filename (non-grid variants).

props.name: emptyText
props.description: Text shown when `items` is empty.

events.name: kai-remove
events.description: A remove button was clicked.

parts.name: preview
parts.doc: The image shown in an attachment’s hover-card preview. Bounded by default (max ~320×256, aspect preserved) so a large image never blows up the card — raise or lower the cap from outside.

props.name: src
props.description: Image URL/data-URI. When absent, the `fallback` initials show instead.

props.name: alt
props.description: Alt text for the image. Defaults to `fallback`.

props.name: fallback
props.description: Short text shown when there's no image — usually initials (e.g. "JD", "AI").

props.name: size
props.description: Size token: `sm` | `md` (default) | `lg`.

props.name: variant
props.description: `default` (muted pill) · `count` (compact number badge) · `citation` (filled primary, for inline citation markers). Defaults to `default`.

parts.name: badge
parts.doc: The badge pill. Restyle its background, color, or shape; the `variant` prop (default/count/citation) sets the defaults.

props.name: variant
props.description: Visual style. `default` (filled), `subtle` (muted text, hover tint — the toolbar icon look), `ghost` (transparent, hover fill), `outline`, or `destructive`. Defaults to `default`.

props.name: size
props.description: Size token. `icon` / `icon-sm` are square (for icon-only buttons); `sm` / `md` / `lg` size text buttons. Defaults to `md`.

props.name: icon
props.description: Leading icon: a named icon (e.g. `"mic"`, `"plus"`), an image URL/data-URI, or plain text. Renders before any slotted label.

props.name: iconTrailing
props.description: Trailing icon, after the label (e.g. `"chevron-down"` for a menu affordance).

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible name. REQUIRED for icon-only buttons (no visible text); ignored when you slot visible text, which already names the button.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disable the button (non-interactive, dimmed).

props.name: full
props.description: Stretch the button to the full width of its container (a block button) — e.g. a card CTA or a stacked action. Attribute: `full`.

props.name: align
props.description: Justify the button's content: `start`, `center` (default), or `end`. Combine with `full` for a full-width, left-aligned button.

props.name: type
props.description: Native button `type`. Defaults to `button` (so it never submits a form).

events.name: kai-click
events.description: The button was activated (pointer or keyboard). Carries no detail. The native `click` also bubbles (composed) for consumers who prefer it.

methods.name: focus
methods.description: Focus the inner `<button>` (host.focus() would focus the wrapper).

methods.name: blur
methods.description: Blur the inner `<button>`.

methods.name: click
methods.description: Programmatically activate the button — runs the same path as a user click and fires kai-click. Forwarding to the inner button means `disabled` is respected automatically.

slots.name: icon
slots.doc: A custom leading icon (any inline SVG, inherits `currentColor`). Wins over the `icon` prop.

parts.name: button
parts.doc: The button element. Restyle radius, padding, colors, or weight from outside; the `variant`/`size` props set the defaults.

props.name: appearance
props.description: Surface treatment: `outlined` (default) | `filled` | `plain` | `accent`. Attribute: `appearance`.

props.name: orientation
props.description: `vertical` (default, media on top) | `horizontal` (media at the start) | `responsive` (horizontal when the card's container is wide enough, else vertical — a container query on the card's own width). Attribute: `orientation`.

props.name: collapse
props.description: The card width below which a `responsive` card collapses to vertical and the footer actions stack. A CSS length; default `28rem`. Attribute: `collapse`.

props.name: dense
props.description: Tighter spacing for dense lists. Attribute: `dense`.

props.name: dismissible
props.description: Show a close (×) that hides the card and emits `kai-dismiss`. Attribute: `dismissible`. Off by default.

props.name: href
props.description: Render the whole card as a link. Attribute: `href`. Wins over `clickable`.

props.name: target
props.description: `target` for the `href` anchor. Attribute: `target`.

props.name: rel
props.description: `rel` for the `href` anchor. Attribute: `rel`.

props.name: clickable
props.description: Make the whole card a button (`role="button"`, Enter/Space, hover affordance) that emits `kai-card-click`. Attribute: `clickable`. Ignored when `href` is set.

events.name: kai-card-click
events.description: A `clickable`/`href` card was activated (click, or Enter/Space).

events.name: kai-dismiss
events.description: The card was dismissed via its × (it also hides itself).

slots.name: media
slots.doc: Full-bleed media (image/video/illustration) at the top (vertical) or start (horizontal). Clipped to the card corners.

slots.name: header
slots.doc: Header content, e.g. a title. Rendered above the body.

slots.name: header-actions
slots.doc: An actions cluster pinned to the end of the header row.

slots.name: footer
slots.doc: Footer content rendered below the body.

slots.name: footer-actions
slots.doc: Action buttons pinned to the end of the footer. Do NOT combine with a clickable/href card (nested interactive).

parts.name: card
parts.doc: The card root (a div, or an a when href is set). Restyle its radius, border, or background; set --kai-card-spacing for padding/gaps (the dense prop sets the compact default).

parts.name: media
parts.doc: The full-bleed media region. Cap or crop it from outside (e.g. a fixed height with object-fit).

parts.name: header
parts.doc: The header row (header content + header-actions). Add a divider or adjust its alignment.

parts.name: body
parts.doc: The default-slot body region.

parts.name: footer
parts.doc: The footer row (footer content + footer-actions).

parts.name: dismiss
parts.doc: The dismiss (×) button shown when dismissible. Recolor or reposition it from outside.

props.name: cards
props.type: undefined | { type: string; id: string; data: unknown; title?: undefined | string; resolution?: undefined | { kind: "action"; action: string; payload?: unknown; at?: undefined | string } | { kind: "submit"; data: unknown; at?: undefined | string } | { kind: "dismissed"; at?: undefined | string } | { kind: "expired"; reason?: undefined | string; at?: undefined | string } }[]
props.description: The stream of card envelopes to render. Set as a JS PROPERTY: `el.cards = [...]`.
props.displayType: undefined | { type: string; id: string; data: unknown; title?: undefined | string; resolution?: undefined | { kind: "action"; action: string; payload?: unknown; at?: undefined | string } | { kind: "submit"; data: unknown; at?: undefined | string } | { kind: "dismissed"; at?: undefined | string } | { kind: "expired"; reason?: undefined | string; at?: undefined | string } }[]

props.name: types
props.description: Optional type→tag overrides/additions (merged over the built-ins). Property: `el.types`. Typed as a plain string map (not the `CardTagMap` alias) so the generated React wrapper inlines it instead of emitting an unresolved named type.

props.name: policy
props.type: undefined | { onSubmit?: undefined | (cardId: string, data: unknown) => void; onAction?: undefined | (cardId: string, action: string, payload?: unknown) => void; onSendPrompt?: undefined | (text: string, opts: { mode: "compose" | "send"; context?: unknown; }) => void; onOpen?: undefined | (url: string, target: "tab" | "artifact") => void; onState?: undefined | (cardId: string, patch: unknown) => void; onDismiss?: undefined | (cardId: string) => void; onReopen?: undefined | (cardId: string) => void; onError?: undefined | (cardId: string, message: string) => void; maxSendPromptMode?: undefined | "compose" | "send" }
props.description: Optional CardPolicy handling child events. Property: `el.policy`.
props.displayType: undefined | { onSubmit?: undefined | (cardId: string, data: unknown) => void; onAction?: undefined | (cardId: string, action: string, payload?: unknown) => void; onSendPrompt?: undefined | (text: string, opts: { mode: "compose" | "send"; context?: unknown; }) => void; onOpen?: undefined | (url: string, target: "tab" | "artifact") => void; onState?: undefined | (cardId: string, patch: unknown) => void; onDismiss?: undefined | (cardId: string) => void; onReopen?: undefined | (cardId: string) => void; onError?: undefined | (cardId: string, message: string) => void; maxSendPromptMode?: undefined | "compose" | "send" }

events.name: kai-card-resolved
events.detail: { cardId: string; resolution: { kind: "action"; action: string; payload?: unknown; at?: undefined | string } | { kind: "submit"; data: unknown; at?: undefined | string } | { kind: "dismissed"; at?: undefined | string } | { kind: "expired"; reason?: undefined | string; at?: undefined | string } }
events.description: A child card transitioned to a resolved/deferred state (an action was chosen, a form/tasks submission landed, or it was dismissed) — re-emitted off the host as a non-bubbling convenience event so a consumer can observe resolution centrally without diffing the cards array. `detail` = `{ cardId, resolution }`. (A `reopen` un-resolves a card and has no `CardResolution`, so it does NOT fire this — observe reopen via the underlying bubbling `kai-card` event.)
events.displayDetail: { cardId: string; resolution: { kind: "action"; action: string; payload?: unknown; at?: undefined | string } | { kind: "submit"; data: unknown; at?: undefined | string } | { kind: "dismissed"; at?: undefined | string } | { kind: "expired"; reason?: undefined | string; at?: undefined | string } }

methods.name: resolve
methods.description: Programmatically resolve a child card by id: set that envelope's `resolution` so the child re-renders into its read-only/resolved view — the imperative twin of the consumer mutating the cards array. No-op for an unknown id.

methods.name: dismiss
methods.description: Collapse a card to its re-openable stub from the host side — convenience for `resolve(cardId, { kind: 'dismissed' })`.

methods.name: getCard
methods.description: Return the live child element node for a card id (or null) so consumers can call that card's own methods (focus/expand/…) without a shadow-DOM query.

props.name: steps
props.type: { label: string; content?: undefined | string; id?: undefined | string }[]
props.description: The reasoning steps. Set as a JS property. Compound sub-parts collapse to this one data model (Route 1). Each `{ label, content?, id? }`.
props.typeShape: { label: string; content?: undefined | string; id?: undefined | string }[]
props.displayType: { label: string; content?: undefined | string; id?: undefined | string }[]

props.name: type
props.description: Open mode: `'multiple'` (default — any number of steps open at once) or `'single'` (at most one open; opening a step closes the others).

props.name: value
props.description: Controlled open step key(s). When set, it WINS over user interaction (the consumer owns the open set). String in `single` mode, string[] in `multiple` mode. Set as a JS property.

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: Uncontrolled INITIAL open step key(s) — seeds which steps render expanded. Ignored once `value` is provided. Set as a JS property.

events.name: kai-value-change
events.description: The open set changed — by user click OR an expand()/collapse()/toggle() call. `value` is a string in `single` mode, a string[] in `multiple` mode. (Maps Radix Accordion's onValueChange.)

methods.name: expand
methods.description: Open one step's detail by index, or — with no arg — ALL steps. In `single` mode opening one step closes the others (expand-all keeps the last).

methods.name: collapse
methods.description: Close one step's detail by index, or — with no arg — ALL steps.

methods.name: toggle
methods.description: Flip one step's open state by index.

props.name: messages
props.type: { id: string; role: "user" | "assistant"; content: string; reasoning?: undefined | { text: string; label?: undefined | string }; tools?: undefined | { type: string; state: "input-streaming" | "input-available" | "output-available" | "output-error"; input?: undefined | Record<string, unknown>; output?: undefined | Record<string, unknown>; toolCallId?: undefined | string; errorText?: undefined | string }[]; attachments?: undefined | { id: string; type: "file" | "source-document"; filename?: undefined | string; mediaType?: undefined | string; url?: undefined | string; title?: undefined | string }[]; actions?: undefined | ("copy" | "like" | "dislike" | "regenerate" | "edit" | { id: string; label: string; icon?: undefined | string; tooltip?: undefined | string })[]; avatar?: undefined | { src?: undefined | string; fallback?: undefined | string; alt?: undefined | string }; feedback?: undefined | "like" | "dislike" }[]
props.description: The full message thread to render, newest last. Each entry carries its role, content, and optional reasoning/tools/attachments/actions. Set as a JS property (`el.messages = [...]`).
props.typeShape: { id: string; role: "user" | "assistant"; content: string; reasoning?: undefined | { text: string; label?: undefined | string }; tools?: undefined | { type: string; state: "input-streaming" | "input-available" | "output-available" | "output-error"; input?: undefined | Record<string, unknown>; output?: undefined | Record<string, unknown>; toolCallId?: undefined | string; errorText?: undefined | string }[]; attachments?: undefined | { id: string; type: "file" | "source-document"; filename?: undefined | string; mediaType?: undefined | string; url?: undefined | string; title?: undefined | string }[]; actions?: undefined | ("copy" | "like" | "dislike" | "regenerate" | "edit" | { id: string; label: string; icon?: undefined | string; tooltip?: undefined | string })[]; avatar?: undefined | { src?: undefined | string; fallback?: undefined | string; alt?: undefined | string }; feedback?: undefined | "like" | "dislike" }[]
props.displayType: { id: string; role: "user" | "assistant"; content: string; reasoning?: undefined | { text: string; label?: undefined | string }; tools?: undefined | { type: string; state: "input-streaming" | "input-available" | "output-available" | "output-error"; input?: undefined | Record<string, unknown>; output?: undefined | Record<string, unknown>; toolCallId?: undefined | string; errorText?: undefined | string }[]; attachments?: undefined | { id: string; type: "file" | "source-document"; filename?: undefined | string; mediaType?: undefined | string; url?: undefined | string; title?: undefined | string }[]; actions?: undefined | ("copy" | "like" | "dislike" | "regenerate" | "edit" | { id: string; label: string; icon?: undefined | string; tooltip?: undefined | string })[]; avatar?: undefined | { src?: undefined | string; fallback?: undefined | string; alt?: undefined | string }; feedback?: undefined | "like" | "dislike" }[]

props.name: value
props.type: undefined | string | ({ type: "text"; text: string } | { type: "entity"; entity: { kind: string; id: string; label: string; icon?: undefined | string; promptText?: undefined | string; data?: undefined | Record<string, unknown> } })[]
props.description: Value of the input. A **string** is controlled (the host owns the text and updates it on `kai-value-change`). A **ComposerDoc** is a one-time seed that pre-populates pills; the user then edits freely. Leave unset for uncontrolled.
props.displayType: undefined | string | ({ type: "text"; text: string } | { type: "entity"; entity: { kind: string; id: string; label: string; icon?: undefined | string; promptText?: undefined | string; data?: undefined | Record<string, unknown> } })[]

props.name: placeholder
props.description: Placeholder text shown in the empty input.

props.name: loading
props.description: When true, shows the loading/streaming state and disables submit (use while awaiting the assistant's reply).

props.name: suggestions
props.description: Starter prompts shown above the input when the thread is empty. Clicking one follows `suggestionMode`. Set as a JS property.

props.name: suggestionMode
props.description: What clicking a suggestion does: `'submit'` (default) sends it immediately as if typed and submitted; `'fill'` just places it in the input.

props.name: persistSuggestions
props.description: Keep suggestions visible after the conversation starts. By default suggestions are conversation starters and hide once `messages` is non-empty; set this to keep them always shown. Default false.

props.name: proseSize
props.description: Body/prose font scale for rendered markdown (`'xs' | 'sm' | 'base' | 'lg'`). Defaults to `'sm'`.

props.name: codeTheme
props.description: Shiki theme name for syntax-highlighted code blocks (e.g. `'github-dark-dimmed'`).

props.name: codeHighlight
props.description: Enable Shiki syntax highlighting in code blocks. Turn off to render plain `<pre>` blocks (lighter, no highlighter load). Default true.

props.name: chatTitle
props.description: Optional header title shown on the left of the header.

props.name: models
props.type: undefined | { id: string; name: string; provider?: undefined | string; description?: undefined | string; group?: undefined | string }[]
props.description: Optional model list. When set (>1 model) a ModelSwitcher is shown in the header and a `kai-model-change` event fires on selection.
props.displayType: undefined | { id: string; name: string; provider?: undefined | string; description?: undefined | string; group?: undefined | string }[]

props.name: currentModel
props.description: The currently selected model id (pairs with `models`).

props.name: context
props.type: undefined | { usedTokens: number; maxTokens: number; inputTokens?: undefined | number; outputTokens?: undefined | number; estimatedCost?: undefined | number }
props.description: Optional context-window token usage. When set, a Context token meter is shown in the header.

props.name: scrollButton
props.description: Show the scroll-to-bottom button inside the scroll area. Default true.

props.name: headerStart
props.description: Whether the host has `slot="header-start"` content (left of the title) — set by the `<kai-chat>` facade so a custom control forces the header open.

props.name: headerEnd
props.description: Whether the host has `slot="header-end"` content (right of the controls).

props.name: headerFull
props.description: REPLACE — full custom header in place of the built-in title/model/context bar.

props.name: sidebar
props.description: INJECT — left sidebar column (e.g. a conversation list / your own nav).

props.name: empty
props.description: REPLACE — custom zero-state rendered in the message area while the thread is empty (replaces the empty message list only; the composer and its suggestions still render).

props.name: composer
props.description: REPLACE — full custom composer in place of the built-in prompt input. The projected content wires its own submit (the data-flow boundary).

props.name: composerActions
props.description: INJECT — accessory row just above the composer (e.g. extra actions).

props.name: footer
props.description: INJECT — footer row below the composer (disclaimers, token meter, …).

props.name: search
props.description: Show a Search (Globe) button in the input toolbar; fires a `search` event.

props.name: voice
props.description: Show a Voice (Mic) button in the input toolbar; fires a `voice` event.

props.name: triggers
props.type: undefined | { char: string; kind: string; items?: undefined | { id: string; label: string; icon?: undefined | string; description?: undefined | string; group?: undefined | string; kind?: undefined | string; promptText?: undefined | string; data?: undefined | Record<string, unknown> }[] }[]
props.description: Rich entity triggers — each `{ char, kind, items }` opens a caret-anchored menu that inserts an atomic pill (`/` skills, `@` agents/plugins). Set as a JS property; forwarded to the input.
props.displayType: undefined | { char: string; kind: string; items?: undefined | { id: string; label: string; icon?: undefined | string; description?: undefined | string; group?: undefined | string; kind?: undefined | string; promptText?: undefined | string; data?: undefined | Record<string, unknown> }[] }[]

props.name: kindIcons
props.description: Default icon per entity kind (kind → image src) for pills/menu items.

props.name: actionsReveal
props.description: Whether each message's action bar is always visible (`'always'`, default) or only revealed on hover of that message row (`'hover'`).

events.name: kai-attachments-change
events.detail: { attachments: { id: string; type: "file" | "source-document"; filename?: undefined | string; mediaType?: undefined | string; url?: undefined | string; title?: undefined | string }[] }
events.description: The staged attachments changed (file added or removed). Carries the full current list so a consumer can react in real time.
events.displayDetail: { attachments: { id: string; type: "file" | "source-document"; filename?: undefined | string; mediaType?: undefined | string; url?: undefined | string; title?: undefined | string }[] }

events.name: kai-message-action
events.detail: { messageId: string; action: string; state?: undefined | "on" | "off" }
events.description: An action button on a message was clicked. `action` is the built-in name or custom id. `state` is present only for the toggleable feedback votes: `'on'` when a like/dislike is set, `'off'` when re-tapped to clear.
events.displayDetail: { messageId: string; action: string; state?: undefined | "on" | "off" }

events.name: kai-model-change
events.description: The header model switcher changed.

events.name: kai-search
events.description: The Search button was clicked.

A plain `<kai-resizable-item collapsed>` in React/Solid/Vue/Svelte collapses the panel at the first render; `hidden` does not, because a JSX boolean sets neither the `hidden` attribute nor the IDL property on a custom element, so the parent never sees it. props.description: Row height for `layout="list"`: `'md'` (default) or `'lg'` for taller rows. Chips are unaffected.

props.name: layout
props.description: Layout: `'chips'` (default) renders a wrapping row of rounded pills; `'list'` renders a vertical, full-width "Ideas for you" list — each row is left-aligned with a leading `icon`, a label, and a hover background.

props.name: block
props.description: Full-width left-aligned rows instead of pills.

props.name: highlight
props.description: Substring to highlight within each suggestion.

events.name: kai-select
events.description: A suggestion was clicked.

props.name: checked
props.description: Controlled checked state — settable and reflected to the `checked` attribute. `el.checked = true` (or `<kai-switch checked>`) drives it; the toggle UI updates it and fires `kai-change`. Read `el.checked` for live state.

props.name: defaultChecked
props.description: Initial checked state on mount (uncontrolled seed). Bare attribute (`<kai-switch default-checked>`) turns it on.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disable interaction.

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible label.

props.name: name
props.description: Form-control name (paired with `value`).

props.name: value
props.description: Submitted value when checked (paired with `name`). Defaults to `'on'`.

events.name: kai-change
events.description: The toggle changed.

methods.name: toggle
methods.description: Flip the switch and fire `kai-change` (no-op while disabled).

methods.name: focus
methods.description: Focus the inner `role="switch"` button (the host element can't reach it).

props.name: items
props.type: undefined | { id: string; label?: undefined | string; icon?: undefined | string; disabled?: undefined | false | true }[]
props.description: Tabs to render. Set as a JS property, not an HTML attribute.
props.displayType: undefined | { id: string; label?: undefined | string; icon?: undefined | string; disabled?: undefined | false | true }[]

props.name: value
props.description: Controlled selected id. Set as a JS property (or the `value` attribute); drive it from your app in response to `kai-tab-change`. Omit for uncontrolled.

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: Initial selected id when uncontrolled (use the `default-value` attribute in plain HTML).

props.name: variant
props.description: `segmented` (default, a pill group) or `underline` (an underlined row).

props.name: block
props.description: Stretch the strip to full width, each tab sharing the space equally.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disable the whole strip.

events.name: kai-tab-change
events.description: A tab was selected (click, Enter/Space, or arrow-key move). `value` is the item's id.

methods.name: select
methods.description: Select a tab by id (fires `kai-tab-change`). Ignores unknown/disabled ids.

methods.name: focus
methods.description: Focus the active tab (or the first focusable tab).

parts.name: tablist
parts.doc: The tab strip container (role="tablist"). Restyle its gap, padding, background, or radius from outside; the `variant` prop sets the segmented/underline defaults.

parts.name: tab
parts.doc: A single tab button. Restyle from outside; the active tab carries a `[data-active]` attribute, so target `::part(tab)[data-active]` for the selected look.

props.name: data
props.description: The tasks definition (the CardEnvelope.data). Set as a JS PROPERTY: `el.data = { tasks:[…], selectAll, confirmLabel, … }`. Import `TasksCardData` from `@kitn.ai/ui` for the full shape.

props.name: cardId
props.description: Stable card id correlating every emitted CardEvent. Attribute: `card-id`.

props.name: heading
props.description: Heading rendered in the card chrome (= CardEnvelope.title). Attribute: `heading`.

props.name: resolution
props.description: Set when the user resolved this card; renders the read-only view. Property: `el.resolution = { kind:'submit', data:{ selected:[…] } }`.

props.name: value
props.description: Controlled selection (task ids; JS property). When set, it wins over local state.

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: Uncontrolled initial selection (task ids; JS property), overlaying per-task `checked`.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Freeze the whole list + Confirm. Attribute: `disabled`.

props.name: readonly
props.description: Display-only: rows can't be toggled and show the default cursor (no pointer, hover, or focus affordances). Keeps the look as-is. Attribute: `readonly`.

events.name: kai-value-change
events.description: The selection changed on a toggle — the selected ids in input order.

methods.name: select
methods.description: Set the checked task ids (local-only, no emit), respecting disabled/max. With no arg, select all toggleable rows.

methods.name: toggle
methods.description: Toggle one task by id, honoring the max gate (no `checked` = flip).

methods.name: send
methods.description: Confirm the current selection — emits the `submit` CardEvent + resolves (only when the min/max gate passes). Named `send`, not `submit`.

methods.name: focus
methods.description: Focus the task group (select-all checkbox if shown, else the first row).

methods.name: dismiss
methods.description: Trigger the dismiss path (emit `dismiss` + collapse to the re-openable stub).

methods.name: reopen
methods.description: Re-open a dismissed card from its stub (emit `reopen`).

props.name: text
props.description: The text to shimmer.

props.name: as
props.description: Element tag to render as (default `span`).

props.name: duration
props.description: Animation duration in seconds.

props.name: spread
props.description: Gradient spread (5–45).

props.name: text
props.description: The shimmering label, e.g. "Thinking…".

props.name: stoppable
props.description: When true, show a "stop" affordance that fires a `stop` event.

props.name: stopLabel
props.description: Label for the stop affordance.

events.name: kai-stop
events.description: The "stop / answer now" affordance was clicked.

props.displayType: undefined | { id: string; role: "user" | "assistant"; content: string; reasoning?: undefined | { text: string; label?: undefined | string }; tools?: undefined | { type: string; state: "input-streaming" | "input-available" | "output-available" | "output-error"; input?: undefined | Record<string, unknown>; output?: undefined | Record<string, unknown>; toolCallId?: undefined | string; errorText?: undefined | string }[]; attachments?: undefined | { id: string; type: "file" | "source-document"; filename?: undefined | string; mediaType?: undefined | string; url?: undefined | string; title?: undefined | string }[]; actions?: undefined | ("copy" | "like" | "dislike" | "regenerate" | "edit" | { id: string; label: string; icon?: undefined | string; tooltip?: undefined | string })[]; avatar?: undefined | { src?: undefined | string; fallback?: undefined | string; alt?: undefined | string }; feedback?: undefined | "like" | "dislike" }[]

props.description: Show a typing indicator on the pending assistant turn — set while awaiting the assistant's reply.

props.name: proseSize
props.description: Body/prose font scale for rendered markdown (`'xs' | 'sm' | 'base' | 'lg'`). Defaults to `'sm'`.

props.name: codeTheme
props.description: Shiki theme name for syntax-highlighted code blocks (e.g. `'github-dark-dimmed'`).

props.name: codeHighlight
props.description: Enable Shiki syntax highlighting in code blocks. Turn off to render plain `<pre>` blocks (lighter, no highlighter load). Default true.

props.name: actionsReveal
props.description: Whether each message's action bar is always visible (`'always'`, default) or only revealed on hover of that message row (`'hover'`).

props.name: scrollButton
props.description: Show the scroll-to-bottom button inside the scroll area. Default true.

props.name: class
props.description: Extra classes applied to the thread's inner root.

events.description: A message's action button was clicked. `action` is the built-in name (`copy` / `like` / `dislike` / `regenerate` / `edit`) or a custom id. `state` is present only for the toggleable feedback votes: `'on'` when a like/dislike is set, `'off'` when re-tapped to clear.
events.displayDetail: { messageId: string; action: string; state?: undefined | "on" | "off" }

methods.name: scrollToBottom
methods.description: Scroll the message list to the bottom (default `'smooth'`).

props.name: toasts

props.description: The toasts to render. Newest is shown on top. Set as a JS property (array); pass a new array reference to update.
props.typeShape: { id: string; message: string; variant?: undefined | "error" | "info" | "success" | "warning" | "neutral"; appearance?: undefined | "pill" | "card"; inverse?: undefined | false | true; description?: undefined | string; action?: undefined | { label: string; onAction: () => void | false }; duration?: undefined | number; dismissible?: undefined | false | true; target?: undefined | HTMLElement }[]

props.name: position
props.description: Stack anchor: `'top-center'` (default), `'top-right'`, `'bottom-center'`, …

props.name: max
props.description: Max simultaneously-visible toasts; the rest queue. Defaults to `3`.

props.name: stack
props.description: Stacking: 'expanded' (default, full column) | 'collapsed' (Sonner-style pile that expands on hover/focus). Attribute: stack.

props.name: appearance
props.description: Default appearance for this region's toasts: `'pill'` (default, compact) | `'card'` (richer, with a description line). A per-toast `appearance` wins. Attribute: `appearance`.

props.name: inverse
props.description: Default high-contrast inverse treatment for this region's toasts. A per-toast `inverse` wins. Off by default. Attribute: `inverse`.

props.name: target
props.description: Container element to anchor this region to (JS property). Set by the store for a scoped region; unset = the global viewport region.

events.name: kai-action
events.description: A toast's action button was pressed.

events.name: kai-dismiss
events.description: A toast left the stack. `reason` is `'timeout' | 'close' | 'action'`.

props.type: undefined | { type: string; state: "input-streaming" | "input-available" | "output-available" | "output-error"; input?: undefined | Record<string, unknown>; output?: undefined | Record<string, unknown>; toolCallId?: undefined | string; errorText?: undefined | string }
props.description: The tool-call to display. Set as a JS property.

props.description: Initial open state on mount (uncontrolled seed).

props.name: disabled
props.description: Gate the disclosure trigger — programmatic `show()/hide()/toggle()` still work, but the trigger click no longer toggles.

methods.name: hide
methods.description: Close it programmatically.

methods.name: toggle
methods.description: Flip the open state (closes while disabled).

props.name: content
props.description: The hint text shown on hover/focus of the slotted trigger.

props.name: openDelay
props.description: Delay (ms) before the tooltip appears on hover. Defaults to 600. Focus shows it immediately regardless.

props.name: closeDelay
props.description: Delay (ms) before it hides after the pointer leaves. Defaults to 0 (hides immediately).

props.name: placement
props.description: Preferred placement: `'top' | 'bottom' | 'left' | 'right'` (+ optional `-start`/`-end`). Defaults to `'top'`; flips to stay in view.

props.name: open
props.description: Drive/observe open state (Shoelace-style: settable + reflected to the `open` attribute, the element still self-manages on hover/focus). Set `el.open = true`, or `<kai-tooltip open>`; listen for `kai-open-change`.

props.name: defaultOpen
props.description: Initial open state on mount (uncontrolled seed).

props.name: disabled
props.description: Turn the tooltip off while keeping the trigger mounted (hover/focus and `show()` no longer open it).

events.name: kai-open-change
events.description: The tooltip opened or closed (by hover/focus, outside-click, or a method).

methods.name: show
methods.description: Open it programmatically (no-op while disabled).

methods.name: hide
methods.description: Close it programmatically.

methods.name: toggle
methods.description: Flip the open state (closes while disabled).

props.name: transcribe
props.description: Transcriber the host supplies — records audio, returns the text. This is a **function-valued property** (`el.transcribe = async blob => '...'`) because a value-returning callback can't be modelled as a fire-and-forget event.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disable the mic button (non-interactive).

props.name: recognitionLang
props.description: BCP-47 language tag for the native `SpeechRecognition` path (e.g. `en-US`). Attribute: `recognition-lang` (the plain `lang` attribute is reserved by `HTMLElement` and can't be a custom-element property). No effect when `transcribe` is set or the browser lacks SpeechRecognition.

props.name: interim
props.description: Emit live partial transcripts (`kai-transcript-interim`) during native recognition. Attribute: `interim`. No-op on the transcribe/fallback paths.

events.name: kai-audio-captured
events.description: Raw audio captured (before transcription) — for hosts that prefer to handle transcription themselves instead of via the `transcribe` property. Also the unsupported-fallback signal: no `transcribe`, no SpeechRecognition, so only the blob is produced (no text).

events.name: kai-recording-change
events.description: Recording started or stopped — lets the host drive its own UI (waveform, push-to-talk indicator) in sync with the mic. Fires on real transitions only (manual click and programmatic start()/stop()), never on mount.

events.name: kai-transcript-interim
events.description: Live partial transcript during native recognition (only when `interim` is set). Fires repeatedly before the final `kai-transcription`.

events.name: kai-transcription
events.description: Final transcript — the `transcribe` property resolved, OR native `SpeechRecognition` produced final text (no `transcribe` set).

methods.name: start
methods.description: Begin recording programmatically (e.g. push-to-talk bound to a global key). Runs the same getUserMedia path as clicking the mic; no-ops if already recording.

methods.name: stop
methods.description: Stop the in-progress recording, producing the blob (→ kai-audio-captured) and running transcription. Pairs with start() for push-to-talk.

props.name: text
props.description: The utterance to read aloud.

props.name: autoplay
props.description: Speak automatically when `text` is set/changed.

props.name: synthesize
props.description: TTS model seam the host supplies — given text, returns an audio `Blob` to play. This is a **function-valued property** (`el.synthesize = async text => blob`); when set, the native `speechSynthesis` path is bypassed. Mirrors `<kai-voice-input>`'s `transcribe`. A value-returning callback can't be modelled as a fire-and-forget event, hence a property.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disable the button (non-interactive).

events.name: kai-speaking-change
events.description: Playback started or stopped — drive your own UI in sync. Fires on real transitions only (manual click and programmatic speak()/stop()), never on mount.

events.name: kai-synthesized
events.description: The model path (`synthesize`) resolved audio — the raw `Blob` before playback.

methods.name: speak
methods.description: Speak the current `text` (native, or via `synthesize` if set).

methods.name: pause
methods.description: Pause playback (resumable).

methods.name: resume
methods.description: Resume paused playback.

methods.name: stop
methods.description: Stop playback and reset.
